posts.text: You can reach out to them through insta/whatsapp. They are revamping the site and a better experience one will be live soon. Regards, Raj Disc: Invested since last 4 months

posts.author: sanketkulkarni1987
posts.community: L. T. Elevators: Can they lift investors
posts.text: Its a good question. I have been trying to book a slot for Ricardo elevator call back on the Ricardo site. I dont think the site is functional. I am not sure where are they generating these D2C orders from. Is it directly via instagram or some other platform ? But in any case, the site should be functional for prospective customers to connect with them. Other members on this forum can also verify and check

posts.author: Shubham2
posts.community: L. T. Elevators: Can they lift investors
posts.text: image 1498×299 63.8 KB has anyone tracked how did Ricardo managed to scale from 5.5 cr revenue in FY25 to an order run rate of 6 cr per month as claimed by L.T. elevator management?

posts.author: rk1771
posts.community: L. T. Elevators: Can they lift investors
posts.text: The company has come out with a press release. The company plans to increase its number of experience centre to 40, and expecting 6 crores topline each from these centres. The company has started exporing to Malasia. They have received regulatory approval in Australia, and export is likely to commence soon. The company is also making endeavours to serve UAE, Oman, Thailand and Indonasia. LT Press release.pdf (1.2 MB)
